name: Bacon

identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g as-sold basis matches package-label style nutrition and avoids cooked-water-loss distortion.
includes: plain cured sliced bacon as sold

metricProvenance.header: USDA SR Legacy style pork bacon cured unprepared entry, normalized to per 100 g as sold
metricProvenance.saturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.omega3_mg: derived fatty-acid field from the same source family; useful as supporting context, not the main story
metricProvenance.polyunsaturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.cholesterol_mg: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.collagen_g: current value is still a rough calibration-era proxy, not a locked source-backed bacon-specific fact
metricProvenance.essential_amino_acids_score: current value is still a calibration proxy rather than an exact-source amino-acid profile for this chosen bacon form
metricProvenance.nonessential_amino_acids_score: current value is still a calibration proxy rather than an exact-source amino-acid profile for this chosen bacon form
metricProvenance.bioavailability_percent: current value is still a proxy estimate, not a locked canonical percent for this exact bacon entry
metricProvenance.vitamin_a_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_c_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_d_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_e_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_k_dv: same canonical source family as header

pros.title: highly palatable and appealing
pros.explanation: A real-world reason people love it.

pros.title: works as a strong flavour booster
pros.explanation: Useful in meals even in smaller amounts.

pros.title: has strong comfort-food appeal
pros.explanation: Popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

cons.title: sodium is a major drawback
cons.explanation: A central practical health issue for bacon.

cons.title: processing is a major negative
cons.explanation: A key downside now that more processed foods are in scope.

cons.title: rarely improves a meal much on its own
cons.explanation: Usually works more as a flavour add-on than a genuinely strong meat choice.

scoreReadiness.summary: Core raw bacon nutrition is coherent enough for launch scripting, but a few protein-quality style fields are still older calibration proxies rather than locked source facts.
blockers: collagen and protein-quality proxy metrics still need either exact-source backing or deliberate removal
cured bacon product variability means exact sodium-forward real-world burden is broader than this simplified nutrition record captures

sourceNotes: Identity locked to cured pork bacon, unprepared, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with USDA SR Legacy style pork bacon cured unprepared entries.
Raw basis kept intentionally, not cooked bacon values.
